searchLedger.hypothesis: Missing live shard with sentinel does not attach the quarantined shard

searchActions.result: When the live shard path is missing, run_vector_shard_integrity_probe_at_path returns ok at 507-508. With a pending sentinel, attachActiveVectorShard resumes repair at 1410-1411 and then ATTACHes the canonical shardPath at 1414-1415, not sentinel.quarantinePath.

searchLedger.rationale: For the normal quarantine-then-restart case, the corrupt file is at quarantinePath and the canonical shardPath is missing. The reviewed path opens the canonical path, creating/using an empty rebuilding shard, and does not attach the quarantined file.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The code never uses sentinel.quarantinePath as the attach target.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Degraded seed and completion clear use matching shard basename on the single-shard path

searchActions.result: recordVectorShardQuarantined stores lastShard as basenameOrNull(input.shardPath) at 230. recordVectorShardRebuildCompleted computes completedShard the same way at 246 and clears activeJobId when degradedVectorHealth.lastShard === completedShard at 250-252.

searchLedger.rationale: For the reviewed boot-resume path, resume and completion both use the same canonical shardPath, so the later clear can match the degraded seed.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No degraded-key mismatch found for the normal single active shard/profile path.

title: Stale repair-pending sentinel unconditionally re-repairs a healthy shard
evidence: Boot reads sentinel presence for the current shard at 1356 via read_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel, which only checks for the file and parses reason/quarantinePath at 1058-1071. If the shard probe passes, pendingRepairSentinel still calls resume_vector_shard_repair_from_sentinel at 1410-1411, which records quarantine and schedules rebuild at 1098-1103. clear_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel is best-effort and leaves the sentinel on unlink failure at 1078-1088.
rationale: Concrete boot scenario: a repair completed and produced a valid active shard, but the process crashed before sentinel clear, or fs.unlinkSync failed during clear. The next boot sees a healthy shard plus a stale sentinel; this code does not validate sentinel freshness against the shard or consume/mark the sentinel before scheduling, so it resumes repair on a healthy shard. The prompt classifies false-positive repair on a healthy shard as P0.

title: Sentinel resume has no per-shard idempotence guard
evidence: Every resume_vector_shard_repair_from_sentinel call reaches schedule_vector_shard_rebuild at 1103. schedule_vector_shard_rebuild only defers startVectorShardRepairReindex at 567-570; it does not claim the sentinel, clear it, lock the shard, or record an in-flight shard key. attachActiveVectorShard can reach the same resume path while already attached at 1384-1385 or while unattached at 1410-1411.
rationale: Concrete boot scenario: two daemon boots, or a boot retry/repeated attach before the first repair clears the sentinel, both observe the same sentinel and enqueue independent repair jobs for the same shard. Staging makes this bounded for data safety, but it is still a double-schedule race and can produce duplicate rebuild work and degraded-health flapping.
